wiki.summary: “trap house 2016” sees Nettspend rap over a distorted beat, which was produced by frequent collaborator skai and consists of glitchy high-pitched synths, while hard-hitting bass and fast-paced drums distort the instrumental. The title comes as ironic, because the same picture used for the album cover was also took in 2016, as can be seen on Nettspend’s old Twitter acount, from when he was just a kid. The track was first previewed on January 17, 2026, where Nettspend would showcase the track live during his Australia tour. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “trap house 2016” sees Nettspend rap over a distorted beat, which was produced by frequent collaborator skai and consists of glitchy high-pitched synths, while hard-hitting bass and fast-paced drums distort the instrumental. The title comes as ironic, because the same picture used for the album cover was also took in 2016, as can be seen on Nettspend’s old Twitter acount, from when he was just a kid. The track was first previewed on January 17, 2026, where Nettspend would showcase the track live during his Australia tour. A couple of days later, he would post a snippet of the track on his secondary Instagram account. On February 16, Nettspend would preview the track again as a trailer on his alternative account before the album’s release. Nettspend released “trap house 2016” on March 6, 2026. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
